They’re like “Listen, I want the challenge, game’s too easy. Let me miss the first three clues.” 24 is the first clue. Good luck. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Big Den 948” said gold. 24 karat, it’s a good guess. “Breezy Jet 819” from Lynwood, California put Kobe; it’s definitely a California answer. “Jimmy Flapjacks” said crayons.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: I’LL BE BACK. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I did not mean to say that with a little bit of an accent. Ignore that, that is not a part of the clue. I’ll be back. 3 seconds left. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I’LL BE BACK. “Chloe Quinn 18” said Target. “Arie Schwarzfelder”—I think that might be one of the worst misspellings we’ve ever had for what is not the correct answer anyway.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: THE DAY IS YOUNG. All right, 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 24, I’LL BE BACK, THE DAY IS YOUNG. I’ve heard about the night and its youth, but the day being young? 5 seconds left. Can the day rent a car? Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE DAY IS YOUNG. “Jaw Fixer” said sunrise. “Jean Street” said waffles—that is not a good guess. Maybe because it’s a breakfast food?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE. 20 seconds and the clock starts now. Does a sunrise have a plus one? I guess the sun, but that kind of is the rise. If the sunrise ever does come with a plus one, like a second sun, we’re all in so much trouble. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE. “Ants in My Lance” said equation. Always arrives with a plus one, that’s a good guess.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR. 20 seconds starts now. I promise rhymes on clue fives. A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR. Time is up. Let’s find out what’s inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It is FEB 29. LEAP YEAR baby! FEB 29. It’s a very good puzzle.
Clue 5: "A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR." It comes every four years... the next one is 2028. 8,648 got it right.
Clue 4: "ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE." Whenever leap year comes it brings one extra day with it. 120 got it right.
Clue 3: "THE DAY IS YOUNG." Because of leap year the 29th day of February is always four times as young as any other day. 135 got it right.
Clue 2: "I'LL BE BACK." It leaves us but always comes back every four years. 86 got it right.
Clue 1: "TWENTY-FOUR." There are 24 hours in a day and those 24 hours are what make a leap year. 12 got it right.
Clue 1: 12 winners at $833.33 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two is worth $20,000. Five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let us load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here’s your first clue: CAN GET TESTY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. CAN GET TESTY. These answers could be interesting. 5 seconds. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: CAN GET TESTY. “Ry Master 11” said Batman. “John L Hart” said baby. “Grape Shelly” said my wife. Good luck tonight, “Grape Shelly.”

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: PHOTO FINISH. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH. I’ve seen your tweets at me; some of you can get testy, but PHOTO FINISH rules it out. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: PHOTO FINISH. “Florugo” from Antioch, Illinois said Scandron. “Just Jeremy 95” said sepia. CPI like sepia tone? It’s a photo finish.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR. 20 seconds starts now. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Pickle Juice 33” from Hallstead said MRI. “Gnarly Doily” said police lineup. Seeing who’s there, who’s not there.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR, THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. 5 seconds left. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. “Glowy Earbuds 354” said Krispy Kreme. Someone else said potato.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: WHERE ONE MUST GO FOR A DRIVER’S LICENSE TO SHOW. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Good luck ladies and gentlemen. WHERE ONE MUST GO FOR A DRIVER’S LICENSE TO SHOW, THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR, PHOTO FINISH, CAN GET TESTY. Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was the DMV. Yay, the DMV! Everyone’s favorite place.
Clue 5: "WHERE ONE MUST GO, FOR A DRIVER'S LICENSE TO SHOW." Well that one was kind of a gimme. 8,038 got it right.
Clue 4: "THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU." The waiting time is notorious at the DMV and it really does take a toll on you. 2,538 got it right.
Clue 3: "EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR." You have to pass a vision exam every so often but it is not exactly a doctor's office. 1,536 got it right.
Clue 2: "PHOTO FINISH." You finish your visit by taking a photo when you're getting your license done. 458 got it right.
Clue 1: "CAN GET TESTY." You often have to take a test while there. And people aren't always in the best mood and can be testy. 163 got it right.
Clue 1: 163 winners at $122.69 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Fledge Mholland, Tiny Pegasus, and 161 others! A special thanks to our friends at Inkind for today’s fantastic offer. Check your win history to get your voucher in the app. We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow! Good night!

clues: THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE
MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER
A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. Hey hey hey hey hey. Sit down, sit down everybody! Before we get started on today’s puzzles we have to celebrate an important milestone. As of yesterday, listen to this: Best Guess Live players have won unbelievably over $2 million!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That’s incredible! Just for thinking outside the box and typing in correct answers, we have put $2 million into the pockets of you, our players, and you seem to be thrilled about it. Congratulations again to all those winners, and let’s add another $15,000 to the total right now. Here’s how this works.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I will give you five clues. Remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think, people!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue I’ll give you 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you’re the only one to get it right on the earliest clue you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video. You just saw what we do with it: we share it! All right, let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one: $5,000. Load that crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Well, here comes the clues to help you guess.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. The clock starts now. 20 seconds is on the clock. A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. I can’t believe we’ve already given $2 million. “Taylor Maid 78” says an exorcism. “Silent Zone 258” said yoga. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your second clue: SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS. And the clock starts now. So the first clue was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, the second clue was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS. Think about that. Almost 10,000 submissions. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Moltton Tricycle 545” said detention. You don’t volunteer for detention. “Fave Moonfall 93” said fishing. I have a friend who thinks it’s spiritual and I’ve never volunteered to go with him.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your third clue: BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION. The clock starts now. A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, and your third clue is BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION. “Tori Wines 760” said allergies. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Tori Wines” said allergies. Well, you don’t volunteer for allergies. You could get breathless because you can’t breathe.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is clue number four: COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE. And the clock starts now. Clue number one was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, clue number two was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, clue number three was BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION, and the last clue I gave you was COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Pixel Princess 003” said the flu. “Boats Shoe Gary 112” said getting dumped. Is that spiritual? I know what you’re thinking!
Howie Mandel:
Clue 5: THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE. And the clock starts now. That was

Clue 5: for $5,000. Clue one was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, clue two was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, clue three was BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION, clue four was COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE, and the final clue was THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE. Time is up.
Over 42,000 guesses are in.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is a SNEEZE. I’m a germaphobe. Let’s find out who is taking home the $5,000.
Clue 5: "THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE." That's exactly what a sneeze is. 5,539 got it right.
Clue 4: "COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE." It's probably one of the most common things and you are going to want to grab a tissue. 6,994 got it right.
Clue 3: "BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION." You know that feeling when you have to sneeze it comes. 238 got it right.
Clue 2: "SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS." You don't really volunteer to sneeze you can't control it it's the opposite of volunteering. 10 got it right.
Clue 1: "A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE." What happens after you sneeze what do people say to you bless you That's spiritual. 38 got it right.
Clue 1: 38 winners at $131.57 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let’s play round two. The prize doubles to $10,000! Let’s load that crystal ball one more time. It’s fully loaded now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your first clue. First clue: INCOMING. And the clock starts now. You heard me: INCOMING! Time is up. “Delicate Anvil 535” said cannonball. “Joel 037” said a toilet.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your second clue: MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER. The clock starts now. First clue INCOMING, second clue MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER. Over 13,500 guesses on the first one. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Tiny Helicopter 645” says Tinder. “Luffy” said dynamite. How do you come up with these handles?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your third clue: COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION. The clock starts now. So the first clue is INCOMING, the second clue is MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, and my third clue was COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Cute Cat 202” says birthday cake. “Dark Raven 666” says email.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER. And the clock starts now. That’s a good riddle. INCOMING, MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION, and MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: “Littlefoot 88” said a ring. “Glowy Earbuds 34” said root beer. I’m confused by you!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your final clue for $10,000: A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY. The clock starts now. INCOMING, MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION, MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER, and the final clue A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY. Time is up. Let’s find out what is in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s a TABLE LEAF. A TABLE LEAF! Randy just handed me a TABLE LEAF.
Clue 5: "A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY." You always got to make room for the leaf that's when you put it out. 5,329 got it right.
Clue 4: "MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER." Tables are often made from wood but it's named after what the leaf. 204 got it right.
Clue 3: "COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION." When you have a special occasion it comes out when it's over it goes away.
Clue 2: "MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER." For it to fit you got to match one side with the other. You have to match this side with that side.
Clue 1: "INCOMING." How many times you've been sitting around the table you're ready to eat there's always somebody incoming.
Clue 4: 204 winners at $49.01 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That’s it for this edition of Best Guess Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Hunter will be here next time to help close out the week. Don’t forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN'T MOVE
WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY
CORALS GALORE THAT SHIELD THE AUSSIE SHORE
WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST
AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello everybody! How are we doing? I’m your host Hunter March. It is Friday and we have yet another great deal for everyone who plays our second round today. Think of it as a bonus. That’s just our way of saying thank you for being a part of the Best Guess Live community. Or since I’m always sending you money, I should call you the Best Guess Live family.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Today’s deal is from RDX Sports, the standard setters in performance and innovation for combat sports. So if you are into anything from yoga to Brazilian jiu-jitsu to MMA—and I know that is almost all of our audience—you’ll want to stick around for that deal. And as always, we also have $15,000 up for grabs.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think, keep you on your feet!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. So for everyone that didn’t know how to spell TABLE LEAF or didn’t know what it was yesterday, that’s on you! Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Not giving you a lot of time to think! 5 seconds left. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE. “Hot Crouton” put Styx—the band Styx! Could you imagine how mad people would be if it was Styx? “Dinkus McCoy” said Batman. “Alpha Ash” put Hades. “Kev” said Vecna—really appealing to our bosses there.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN’T MOVE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Over 36,000 people playing right now. If I were guessing, I’d say Grandpa Joe from Charlie and the Chocolate Factory. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN’T MOVE. “Show Me Money 2” said tree. “Schnaples” said seed. “Just In Time” said lava lamp.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. If we put quotes in something, it’s meant to make you think! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Amazing Larry” said skydive chess. No.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: A LAND DOWN UNDER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. One more after this and then we’ve got $10,000 to give away. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A LAND DOWN UNDER. “Wy Leg 825” said Atlantis. Hitting a lot of clues, but is it correct?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: CORALS GALORE THAT SHIELD THE AUSSIE SHORE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let’s go over them one last time. FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE, BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN’T MOVE, WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY, A LAND DOWN UNDER, and CORALS GALORE THAT SHIELD THE AUSSIE SHORE. Time’s up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was the GREAT BARRIER REEF. Of course it was!
Clue 5: "CORALS GALORE THAT SHIELD THE AUSSIE SHORE." Well that's kind of exactly what it is. 1,341 got it right.
Clue 4: "A LAND DOWN UNDER." It's a whole landscape just underneath Australia. 93 got it right.
Clue 3: "WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY." You check your buddy's gear before you dive and Australians call each other mate. 349 got it right.
Clue 2: "BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN'T MOVE." Home to lots of life and is alive itself but does not move. 131 got it right.
Clue 1: "FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE." It is an entirely different ecosystem under the ocean than ours which is on land. 15 got it right.
Clue 1: 15 winners at $333.33 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Everyone who submits an answer gets a $20 digital gift card from RDX Sports. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: INSIDER TRADING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. $10,000 on the line! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: INSIDER TRADING. “Chubby Oatmeal 843” said crypto. “Get Some Now” said Pokemon—trading things inside packets. “Choosy Pickle” said Martha Stewart. We’ve forgiven her!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: THE SUM OF ITS PARTS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Sum of its parts… could be a lot of things. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE SUM OF ITS PARTS. “Ned 512” said everything. “Vulks” said atom. “Yarn Lover” said puzzle.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: A LEGENDARY GIFT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Over 41,000 people playing right now. This could change someone’s life today. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A LEGENDARY GIFT. “Fawn Ammo 250” said kidney—you do become a legend when you give your kidney! “LDB” said DNA.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. “Forest” is spelled a little odd… two R’s? Whatever. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST. “Slim Kyle 032” said Woody. “First Flannel 945” said maze.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Final chance! INSIDER TRADING, THE SUM OF ITS PARTS, A LEGENDARY GIFT, WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST, and AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS. Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A BOX OF CHOCOLATES! A very delicious looking BOX OF CHOCOLATES.
Clue 5: "AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS." You guys get it chocolate's made of cocoa. 415 got it right.
Clue 4: "WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST." Turns out Forrest wasn't spelled wrong because it was Forrest Gump... mama said life is like a box of chocolates. 963 got it right.
Clue 3: "A LEGENDARY GIFT." It has a legend inside boxes of chocolate to tell you where each chocolate is located. 12 got it right.
Clue 2: "THE SUM OF ITS PARTS." All of the chocolate inside added together make it a box of chocolates.
Clue 1: "INSIDER TRADING." You never know what's inside until you try it and then oftentimes you want to trade it for a different one.
Clue 3: 12 winners at $833.33 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Thank you to RDX Sports for the great deal. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend and don’t forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND WON
LOVES THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING
SUPERSTAR FOR SURE; BROKE RECORDS WITH HER ERA TOUR
WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Whoa, almost showed you my tummy there, my bad. I’m your host Hunter March. As always, we have two great puzzles for you today and $15,000 on the line. But I also have a special announcement about tomorrow’s show that I’ll make a little later.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For now, just know that we have an amazing guest coming to visit us in the studio, and I’ve been wrestling with when to tell you who it is… ah, but I’ve pinned down round two as the best place. Actually, that has nothing to do with wrestling—sorry, I thought we were going to continue rule threes. Anyway, it’s a wrestler. Don’t worry about it, I’ll tell you later.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But before you solve that puzzle, let’s solve the round one puzzle and win some money. Here’s how it works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’ll load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, all the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit your answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. All right, I can think of a lot of things that have sold well, but what is it today? Times up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. “Gina Marie 7” from Tucson said the Bible. “Sloppy Grass 401” said wings. “Jim Dandy” said gasoline. It’s an eclectic group!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND ONE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let’s see if that applies to wings or the Bible. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND ONE. “Nerdy Suitcase” said the East. “Ruby Doobydoo” said hay—ambushed by the “West-and-one,” hay? That’s unbelievably good.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: LOVE’S THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I don’t even know how to take that. I hope this is starting to make sense. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Awful Guesser 1” guessed diamond. “Chatty Monster 777” said sunset—loves the opposite of a loose beginning, an ending?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Sunset has never altered my clothing quickly! 5 seconds left. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY. “Laboo 67 Mango” said zipper. “Misty Noodles” said exotic dancer.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: THIS SUPERSTAR FOR SURE BROKE RECORDS WITH HER TOUR. 20 seconds starts now. I promise rhymes on clue fives. Good luck. If you don’t know what this is, I think legally you should! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was TAYLOR SWIFT! Yes!
Clue 5: "SUPERSTAR FOR SURE; BROKE RECORDS WITH HER ERA TOUR." Broke records with her eras tour. 8,321 got it right.
Clue 4: "ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY." A Taylor alters clothing and Swift means quickly. 1,182 got it right.
Clue 3: "LOVES THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING." Well she's engaged to a tight end!. 687 got it right.
Clue 2: "AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND WON." Kanye West ambushed her at the VMAs when she won best female video. 168 got it right.
Clue 1: "BONAFIDE BEST SELLER." 14 of her albums went at least four times platinum. 38 got it right.
Clue 1: 38 winners at $131.57 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, we double the prize to $10,000. Load the crystal ball. I told you I was going to announce tomorrow’s guest: she is a legend of WWE, a Raw and Smackdown champion—Alexa Bliss will be here tomorrow with Howie Mandel! Now, let’s play.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: IN THE BEGINNING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Technically we’re in the beginning of the second puzzle. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: IN THE BEGINNING. “Glitchy Chili” said the end. “Weird Enemy 721” said Nike. “Merman 794” said tadpole.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FEEL THE BEAT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. In the beginning, feel the beat. Nearly 45,000 of you playing, thanks for being here. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Horned Polygon 972” said egg—feel the beat of the egg? “IKumba” said radio.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: STAND AND DELIVER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Clue one: In the beginning. Clue two: Feel the beat. Clue three: Stand and deliver. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Meager Knee 947” said DJ. “Happy Frown 945” said archery—an arrow to the heart.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: AMERICA’S FAVORITE RUN-ON SENTENCE. 20 seconds starts now. For those that don’t know, it’s like a long sentence… I’m trying hard to explain without sounding dumb. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Intense Critic” said Duncan. “Psychotic Eeyore” said Gloria Estefan.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA. 20 seconds starts now. I had to get the cadence right to make it rhyme! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was THE PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE.
Clue 5: "WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA." You say it with the same memorable rhythm every day in school. 4,031 got it right.
Clue 4: "AMERICA'S FAVORITE RUN-ON SENTENCE." It is punctuated only by commas it is a run-on sentence. 377 got it right.
Clue 3: "STAND AND DELIVER." You stand up when you deliver it. 43 got it right.
Clue 2: "FEEL THE BEAT." You put your hand over your heart when you recite it and you feel the beat of your heart.
Clue 1: "IN THE BEGINNING." It is literally the beginning of the school day for many many Americans.
Clue 3: 43 winners at $232.55 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Divine Creek 14, M likes M&M’s, and Extreme Peach! That’s it for this edition. Alexa Bliss will be here tomorrow! We’re here every week at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night!

clues: YOU'LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET
WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON
FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC, MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! I am your host Howie Mandel. I get so excited every time I am standing right here getting ready to help you win a ton of money, but tonight I am next level excited because we have a very special guest announcer. Take it away!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: She is only the second women’s triple crown champion in WWE history, she’s a two-time Smackdown Women’s Champion, a three-time Raw Women’s Champion, and the first woman ever to win both titles. She is an absolute legend of the ring, ladies and gentlemen, Alexa Bliss!

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
lines.text: Hello Best Guess Livers! Oh, it is so great to see you! Thank you for being here. I wouldn’t miss it; I love this show.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Well, I love doing it! It’s even better with you here. Alexa, turn out the lights… no, I can’t, I’m not talking to you. I would imagine there’s half the players playing in the dark at home right now. Okay, you’ve been champion, you’ve won Money in the Bank, how does being on Best Guess Live stack up to any of those moments?

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
lines.text: Well, I’m in the ring, my goal is to pump up the crowd, but here I actually get to help the crowd win money. Pretty cool, right?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Very cool! In fact, I’m about to make it even cooler. If anyone guesses either puzzle on the first clue, I’m going to hit Howie here with a “Twisted Bliss.”

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Oh, the lawyers are saying we can’t do that. And I’m saying please, please don’t! I’m going to make the clues harder. Well, can I do an “Insult to Injury”? No? “Code Red”? Mrs. Mandel says no. I’m just going to go ahead and read the rules.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load that crystal ball. Here is the first clue: RIGHT BACK AT YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: RIGHT BACK AT YOU. “Miles Away 903” said a yo-yo. That’s clever. “Sh 17” said a mirror. “Jump Dogwood 154” said tennis. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: RETINA SWAP. Clock starts now. First clue was Right Back At You. “Moldy Sunshine 033” said contacts. “Moab 508” said wink. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your third clue: DELICIOUS OVER ICE. Clock starts now. You thinking? Trying to think. “Kevin on Earth 142” said hockey, which is delicious over ice. Have you ever tasted hockey? Just my puck. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s the fourth clue: YOU’LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET. Clock starts now. “Jumpy Recliner” thinks an elephant. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON. Clock starts now. Clue one: Right Back At You. Clue two: Retina Swap. Clue three: Delicious Over Ice. Clue four: You’ll Regret Because I Never Forget. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The answer is REVENGE.
Clue 5: "WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON." Revenge of the Nerds Revenge of the Sith and Montazuma's revenge. 3,179 got it right.
Clue 4: "YOU'LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET." If someone is set on revenge they hold that grudge. 483 got it right.
Clue 3: "DELICIOUS OVER ICE." Revenge is a dish best served cold. 53 got it right.
Clue 2: "RETINA SWAP." An eye for an eye classic phrase meaning revenge. 5 got it right.
Clue 1: "RIGHT BACK AT YOU." Revenge is literally when you get back at somebody right back at you. 8 got it right.
Clue 1: 8 winners at $625.00 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round two is worth $10,000. Let’s load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: OBVIOUSLY OUT OF SHAPE. Clock starts now. “Milky Dave 854” said a hippopotamus. For a hippo, that’s their look! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Second clue: DOESN’T PRODUCE PRODUCE. Clock starts now. “Jolly Genius 734” said a supermarket. They sell it, they don’t produce it. “Rusty Level 212” said a zombie. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Third clue: ACRONYMS FEEL RIGHT AT HOME. Clock starts now. “Express Walrest 623” said SOS. “John 84” said IKEA. I always feel out of shape building IKEA furniture. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Fourth clue: AN INCREDIBLE TURN OF EVENTS. Clock starts now. “Twinkle Nose 844” said Elimination Chamber. Still traumatized by that? I was the first one eliminated! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE. Clock starts now. A clue and a plug all in one! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The answer is MADISON SQUARE GARDEN.
Clue 5: "FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC, MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE."  7,728 got it right.
Clue 4: "AN INCREDIBLE TURN OF EVENTS."  9 got it right.
Clue 3: "ACRONYMS FEEL RIGHT AT HOME."  11 got it right.
Clue 2: "DOESN'T PRODUCE PRODUCE."  7 got it right.
Clue 1: "OBVIOUSLY OUT OF SHAPE."  2 got it right.
Clue 1: 2 winners at $5,000.00 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That’s it for Best Guess Live! Alexa, anything you want to say?

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
lines.text: Sure! Catch RAW every Monday night only on Netflix!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I approve that message. We’ll see you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE
THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META
STARTS AT BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE
A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE
A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! How’s it going? I’m your host Hunter March with glasses. This is the game where right answers could mean dramatic money and wrong answers could mean, well, comedy gold. Really, honestly, it’s worth it. Speaking of which, I want to take a second to talk to all of the people out there—and there are a lot of you—who send in ridiculous answers just trying to crack me up. You are sacrificing your chance at big money every night just to get in a cheap joke, and I love you for it! Your kids might not like it when they can’t go to college, but I love it anyway.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For all of you out there actually trying to win the money, I have a brand spanking new $15,000 up for grabs tonight. That’s right, it’s not a Wild Wednesday, but it’s still a good Wednesday. $15,000 in my opinion is still pretty wild. Here’s how you claim your money if you win. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think, meant to get your juices going! After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you’re the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you’ll win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Mystical, magical. Here comes clue number one: THE DEPARTED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE DEPARTED. Interesting. What could it be? I want to say so many things, but I feel like everything is going to be misleading or potentially leading… potentially misleadingly leading. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: THE DEPARTED. “We Woop 818” said Pluto—cuz it was a planet that departed. “Vinnie T” said airport. “Not That Gary” said Blockbuster Video—it’s a movie, they’ve departed.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: NOT A NOVEL NOVEL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE DEPARTED, NOT A NOVEL NOVEL. Interesting, very interesting. You get three more clues if you’re new here, and then we go again for $10,000 on the next puzzle. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Baroque Goof” said remake. “Horned Polygon” said egg—maybe because of Game of Thrones? I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: MANY FRIENDS AREN’T FRIENDS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. MANY FRIENDS AREN’T FRIENDS. That is your third clue. The earlier you answer, the better shot you have at winning some money. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Frothy Bagel” said high school. So true, me and Frothy Bagel were equally losers. “My Tai Bruce” said Leonardo DiCaprio.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE DEPARTED, NOT A NOVEL NOVEL, MANY FRIENDS AREN’T FRIENDS, COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Sloppy Rocket 308” says Superman. “Skilled Bear” said I don’t know. Not a skilled bear! “Real Right” said magazine.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for this puzzle: THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. For those wondering, “meta” does in fact legally rhyme with “meta,” so we’re safe. Time is up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was FACEBOOK.
Clue 5: "THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META." The company that is now Meta started with Facebook. 6,447 got it right.
Clue 4: "COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE." The like button is a famous function of Facebook almost as popular as the poke. 2,257 got it right.
Clue 3: "MANY FRIENDS AREN'T FRIENDS." Just because you're a Facebook friend doesn't mean you're a real life friend. 726 got it right.
Clue 2: "NOT A NOVEL NOVEL." Well it's not actually a book and it's no longer new. 2 got it right.
Clue 1: "THE DEPARTED." Facebook was famously originally called the Facebook so the the departed. 2 got it right.
Clue 1: 2 winners at $2,500.00 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000. Let us load the crystal ball. $10,000 in there! I wish you the best of luck.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: First clue is: EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. I see some fun answers already, which is ironic for such a sad first clue. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. “Hot Crouton” put onion—because it makes you cry. “Rudy Terrier” put Starbucks—maybe because you have to tamp down espresso? “Nifty Slacks” said losing best guess. That is depressing!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: CHANGE IN PLANS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING, CHANGE IN PLANS. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Pe-y” put breakup. “Mike Money Mo” put rain. “Franny Pack” put CPR. “Jenny Arcade” put diapers. At any age, that is a change in plans!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: STARTS AT THE BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Does an onion start at the bottom? Kind of, it’s a root vegetable. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Lefty Alien” said penny. “Whiskey the Cat” said feet. “Canadian Thermostat” said the bottom—because it’s cold.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON’T CELEBRATE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON’T CELEBRATE. If there’s a hole in your onion, that’s usually a bad sign. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Jamming Man” said worm. “Thrice Psalm 26” said grave. It’s between onion and grave!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Fifth and final clue: A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL. 20 seconds starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING, CHANGE IN PLANS, STARTS AT THE BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE, A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON’T CELEBRATE, A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL. Time is up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a FLAT TIRE. The worst type of tire.
Clue 5: "A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL." Well that one speaks for itself. 7,554 got it right.
Clue 4: "A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE." Well no one ever says yeah I caught a flat tire. 459 got it right.
Clue 3: "STARTS AT BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE." No matter where you go on a flat tire the flat part always stays at the bottom. 137 got it right.
Clue 2: "CHANGE IN PLANS." You're on the way to do one thing and now you have to change a tire instead. 265 got it right.
Clue 1: "EXTREMELY DEPRESSING." Obviously it's not only a total bummer but your tire is literally depressed. 93 got it right.
Clue 1: 93 winners at $107.52 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Monates Babs 27, Upper Satchel 961, Rab Kitten Glimmer, and 90 more! That’s it for this edition of Best Guess Live! We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow and don’t forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT
ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST
WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT'S COVERED IN SAND
PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK
EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Wow, and we got a lot of first-time players here. The Real K, welcome in! Grinch 9210, welcome! This is the game where you have to decipher clues to solve a mystery. It’s like Sherlock Holmes but without that weird silly hat. Well, you might actually have a silly hat on, but that’s okay too.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I’m going to give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue I’ll give you 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Load that crystal ball. Tonight I’m going to start with clue number one. I’m feeling a little crazy.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT. The clock starts now. “Chile William 32” said an accordion. I used to go to parties and play air accordion! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your second clue: HAS ITS UPS AND DOWNS. Clock starts now. First clue was the long and short of it; 11,000 of you guessed. “Partly Dowy 243” said his wife Betsy. Good luck to you and Betsy tonight! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: EXTREMELY EDGY. Clock starts now. 12,000 people guessed on the second clue. “Sirly Clown 734” said a U2 groupie because Edge is the guitarist. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST. Clock starts now. “Howy Wowee 41” said Jeff Ross. That’s not me, but I get the roast reference. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your final clue for $5,000: WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT’S COVERED IN SAND. Clock starts now. Clue one: Long and short. Clue two: Ups and downs. Clue three: Extremely edgy. Clue four: Roast from coast to coast. Time is up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is BEACH. I’m a son of a BEACH! That’s a dad joke.
Clue 5: "WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT'S COVERED IN SAND." That's what a beach is it's kind of not even a clue. 4,955 got it right.
Clue 4: "ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST." People love to sunbathe while at the beach you go out there and you roast. 30 got it right.
Clue 3: "EXTREMELY EDGY." The beach is where the edge of the water hits the sand... the land's edge. 5 got it right.
Clue 2: "HAS ITS UPS AND DOWNS." The tide is up... the sun is up the sun is down. 1 got it right.
Clue 1: "THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT." The beach is either long this way or short this way to the water. 7 got it right.
Clue 1: 7 winners at $714.28 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize is doubling to $10,000! Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: MORE THAN WORDS. Clock starts now. “Pure Thermos 373” thinks it’s a song. “Watery Cactus” says sentences. Punctuation counts! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Second clue: PORTION CONTROL. Clock starts now. “Kickball Kevin 24” thinks Ozempic. “Dreamy Music 688” said diet. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: END CREDITS. Clock starts now. First clue was More than Words, second was Portion Control. “Northern Dwights 102” said key best grip boy. That’s very inside Hollywood! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Fourth clue: PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK. Clock starts now. “Warm Popsicle 35” thinks it’s a barista. “Utah Wife” says Cameo. I’m not on Cameo! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE. Clock starts now. 5 seconds left. Time is up. Let’s find out what is in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is A TIP! Randy just handed me one.
Clue 5: "EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE." 20% is customary it is extra money for the job well done. 5,838 got it right.
Clue 4: "PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK." If you don't tip you may get a a little attitude back. 255 got it right.
Clue 3: "END CREDITS." It's the money or credits you give the server at the very end of the meal. 18 got it right.
Clue 2: "PORTION CONTROL." You control the portion of money that they receive... it's all about portion control. 1 got it right.
Clue 1: "MORE THAN WORDS." It's nice to say thank you but money is more meaningful way more than words.
Clue 2: 1 winner at $10,000.00 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I’m getting tipped for this episode! Congratulations to all our winners. We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow and don’t forget to bring your best guest! Good night! Oh look, there’s the credits… I can’t read them, they’re backwards!

clues: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD
TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED
THE RUBY MUPPET YOU'VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD
JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP
A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello, I am your host Hunter March. I want to do some shout outs: Maserati, first-time player; Pro Owl 390, first-time player; and then GMA of 13—one grandma of 13! Pretty young to be a grandma.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It’s a big day here at Netflix Games: Overcooked! All You Can Eat launches today! Guys, genuinely, I love Overcooked. It is a great game to play with friends, especially if you like yelling at those friends. This version lets you play with Netflix-exclusive chefs from Stranger Things and K-pop demon hunters. It’s a great chance to find out if the Demogorgon makes a decent burrito! Shout out to our talented friends at Ghost Town Games and Team 17 for all of their excellent work.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, here’s something else that’s super interesting: the rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues, but remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. Someone called me out for saying “mystical magical” every time, so I’m not going to say that anymore. Look at that boring crystal ball. Anyway, let’s get to clue number one: SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: While we wait, I just want to say thanks for playing. Hi everybody playing on Discord! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING. “Chillfest 9990” from Fort Worth said yelling. “Muggy Mesa 344” said Siri. Someone said the Pope.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. If someone had said the Pope is book smart, I would say yes, but about one book! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART. “Pickle Thawn” said teacher. “Mama Bear 057” said mom. “Normal Hyjinks 198” said Snoop.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD. 20 seconds starts now. I also just realized Snoop Dogg speaks highly of everything—that was a late guess, but I like the effort. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD. “Comm Crew 103” said owl. Someone else said Robin.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This could go in way too many directions. Shout out to the people on TikTok and Reddit, I try to see all of them! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED. My favorite guess was from “Askew Potato” who said Santa. We’ve never done Santa!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: THE RUBY MUPPET YOU’VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD. 20 seconds starts now. I know I said “puppet,” that’s on me, but it was written correctly on screen! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s been inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was ELMO!
Clue 5: "THE RUBY MUPPET YOU'VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD." Well a lot of you guys figured it out on that one. 6,059 got it right.
Clue 4: "TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED." Elmo tickle me Elmo and his entire body is red. 4,916 got it right.
Clue 3: "MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD." Sesame Street is a well-known neighborhood some would say he's the most major player. 310 got it right.
Clue 2: "BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART." He helps kids learn how to read and he lives on Sesame Street and that place is dangerous. 189 got it right.
Clue 1: "SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING." Elmo has an iconically high voice. 34 got it right.
Clue 1: 34 winners at $147.05 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: MIDDLE MANAGEMENT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MIDDLE MANAGEMENT. “Cheeky Tomb 87” said elf. “LA 2368” said target—because the center is the middle? “Bendy Straw” said donut holes.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: GET IT TOGETHER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Do the earlier answers still work? Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: GET IT TOGETHER. “Kanani” from San Diego said glue. “Mary Russell” said Oreo.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TOTALLY WASTED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Keep in mind, “wasted” is spelled maybe differently than you’d assume. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TOTALLY WASTED. “VP Chedda” said Human Resources—man, that guy hates his job. “Jean Claude Van Damme” was another guess—he’s got a snatched waist!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP. 20 seconds starts now. It’s like a call with HR, right? Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Fancy Nancy” said circus animal. Someone else said dog.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP. 20 seconds starts now. Your final 20 seconds. ELMO does not need this! Time’s up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was A BELT.
Clue 5: "A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP." A leather strap that gives pants a wrap. 2,743 got it right.
Clue 4: "JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP." Your belt is a loop and it goes through the hoops of your pants. 3,594 got it right.
Clue 3: "TOTALLY WASTED." While people wear a belt right around their waist totally it's always totally waisted. 6,625 got it right.
Clue 2: "GET IT TOGETHER." The primary job for a belt is to just pull it all together. 1,997 got it right.
Clue 1: "MIDDLE MANAGEMENT." A belt goes right around your midsection middle management. 1,336 got it right.
Clue 1: 1,336 winners at $7.48 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Evil Maiden, Text Farm, and Wyatt Earth. We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend everybody and come back on Monday! Don’t forget to bring your best guest! Good night!]

clues: A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE
IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON'T START FROM SCRATCH
DON'T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK
NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Yes, how exciting! I’m your host Hunter March. As I’m sure you already know by this outfit, it’s National Meatball Day! This wasn’t accidental, people. Your meatball celebrations are probably in full swing right now, so we wanted to give you another reason to celebrate. We’ve got a special deal from our friends at Partana—it’s going out to everyone who just plays in round two.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Partana is a premium Italian food brand rooted in generations of Sicilian traditions, so it’s kind of a meatball day no-brainer. Make sure you stick around for round two to get that great deal. And as always, we also have $15,000 going to some winners! But first, let’s celebrate the rules.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. Then I’ll give you five clues written like riddles to make you think. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. It’s like a glass meatball! Here comes clue number one: ENCORE PERFORMANCE. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Happy Monday! I hope you guys had a lovely weekend. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: ENCORE PERFORMANCE. “Kirk 67” from New York said Eminem—he had an album called Encore. “Chosen Ron” said Rush Hour 2.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: OUT OF SIGHT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. ENCORE PERFORMANCE, OUT OF SIGHT. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OUT OF SIGHT. “Kate PL” said sunset. “Bald Hill” said worm. Crazy pull!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO. 20 seconds starts now. What would a worm’s duo partner be? Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO. “Savage Poncho” said moon—cuz the sun is the other half. “Jx011” said Batman.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: OKAY BOOMER. 20 seconds starts now. If you’ve never heard that phrase, you are the boomer! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OKAY BOOMER. “Savage Otter 821” said Cher. She’s great! “Strong Kraken 891” said meme.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE. 20 seconds starts now. This is pretty easy if you ask me. Good luck! Time’s up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was THUNDER.
Clue 5: "A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE." Well that was your gimme. 10,387 got it right.
Clue 4: "OKAY BOOMER." Thunder makes a booming sound kind of the biggest boom. 199 got it right.
Clue 3: "HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO." The other half is of course Lightning. 106 got it right.
Clue 2: "OUT OF SIGHT." You hear thunder but you do not see it. 38 got it right.
Clue 1: "ENCORE PERFORMANCE." Thunder happens after a lightning strike it is the encore to lightning. 11 got it right.
Clue 1: 11 winners at $454.54 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, prize doubles to $10,000. Everyone who submits an answer gets a voucher for $15 off an order of $75 or more from Partana. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: THINK INSIDE THE BOX. 20 seconds starts now. Very ironic for this show! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THINK INSIDE THE BOX. “Ratic” said cat—they always climb in boxes. “Buff Pencil 385” said Jack. “Little Titan” said the letter O. You are too smart for this show!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON’T START FROM SCRATCH. 20 seconds starts now. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Sleepy Cat” said idea—don’t even worry about building it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: DINNER WITH A VIEW. 20 seconds starts now. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: DINNER WITH A VIEW. “Big Dum Dum” put recipe. I wasn’t calling them a big dum dum, that’s the username! “John With The Wind” said airplane food.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: DON’T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK. 20 seconds starts now. I want to apologize to the people I called dum dums—no one’s guesses are stupid! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: “Big Boss Rick Ross” said sushi. “Dumpster Fire” also a guess.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING. 20 seconds starts now. Your final chance at $10,000! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was A MICROWAVE.
Clue 5: "NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING." Well that's how microwaves work and you don't have to wait that long. 3,516 got it right.
Clue 4: "DON'T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK." Most people do not enjoy the smell of fish cooked from a microwave especially in the office. 5,592 got it right.
Clue 3: "DINNER WITH A VIEW." Most microwaves have that little window for you to see inside of it. 227 got it right.
Clue 2: "IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON'T START FROM SCRATCH." Microwaves are typically used to heat food up not to cook from scratch. 7 got it right.
Clue 1: "THINK INSIDE THE BOX." You heat up your food by literally placing it inside a box. 19 got it right.
Clue 1: 19 winners at $526.31 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Abe Froman 80, Nutty T232, and Ragged Collie 769! Thank you to Partana for today’s deal. See you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: WHO'S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF
FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB
A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Come on! We’re going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. That’s me right here. We’re celebrating the release of the brand-new season of One Piece, which premiered on Netflix today! We know that you’re excited to join the Straw Hat crew on their latest swashbuckling adventure, but I’ll have you know that we’ve been known to buckle a swasher or two ourselves!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s true—we don’t have a zany cast of villains, I can’t stretch my arms like saltwater taffy, and there’s about a 0% chance of a cool sword fight breaking out, but we do have something under our straw hats that makes us worth your while. You know what that is? $15,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So before you go on a treasure hunt with Luffy and the gang, let’s dig up some treasure right here. Here’s how the game works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I’m going to give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles, so they’re meant to make you think. It’s more fun that way!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Well, here comes the clues. Clue number one: MOVING TRUCKS. The clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: MOVING TRUCKS. Is that a pirate? Do I sound like a pirate? No, I don’t. “Lance By Chance 43” thinks it’s Transformers. Those are moving trucks! “Sockhead 29” said a U-Haul. “Tree Branch 22” says a crab—I don’t get that, but you could be right because it’s a riddle! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: ON TOP OF THE WORLD. The clock starts now. First clue was Moving Trucks. “Burnt Omelet 22” said Santa. He’s got that moving truck on top of the globe! “Titanic Mink 464” said skiing. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY. The clock starts now. We’ve already got almost 14,000 guesses in. “Cosmolina” said a dolly. “412” said girdle—it’s supportive when things get tricky! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: WHO’S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF? The clock starts right now. “Chili Sauna 323” thinks it’s Red Lobster. I get it! Surf and turf. I like the way you guys think. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB. The clock starts now. Last chance! MOVING TRUCKS, ON TOP OF THE WORLD, SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY, SURF ON TURF, and FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB. Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is A SKATEBOARD! Look at that!
Clue 5: "FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB." Heel flip and grab are the names of skateboarding tricks. 7,631 got it right.
Clue 4: "WHO'S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF." It's a surfboard but for when you're on land. 1,151 got it right.
Clue 3: "SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY." A skateboard bears your weight when you're doing tricks. 888 got it right.
Clue 2: "ON TOP OF THE WORLD." A skateboard literally holds you a few inches above the Earth's surface. 308 got it right.
Clue 1: "MOVING TRUCKS." Trucks are the metal parts fastened to the bottom of a skateboard. 990 got it right.
Clue 1: 990 winners at $5.05 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000! Let’s load the crystal ball for the second game. Here’s your first clue: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE. The clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE. “Biscuit Guy 7” said the internet. “Just the Ox” said the bathroom. It could be a public restroom! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: HOLD PLEASE. The clock starts now. 18,335 of you took a guess on that first one. “Heather My Nest” said my hand. “Cody 2K” said stocks. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: DON’T SWEAT THE SMALL STUFF. The clock starts now. “Reviserin 25” said yoga. You don’t sweat the small stuff, it’s public or private, and you hold positions. I like the way you think! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: SCHOOL SUPPLIES. The clock starts now. “Endless Whis 305” said a backpack. “Without A Net 904” said gym shorts. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS. The clock starts now. I’m not much of a poet, and my poetry reading is a little bit out. You’re making me sweat my small stuff! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s A LOCKER! And you know what’s weird? This is my locker! How did you get my locker? I’m embarrassed because I love the butterflies.
Clue 5: "A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS." For people in school and your jockstrap and people who are doing sports. 6,673 got it right.
Clue 4: "SCHOOL SUPPLIES." School supplies you with your locker and you can put your supplies in it. 98 got it right.
Clue 3: "DON'T SWEAT THE SMALL STUFF." It holds both small items and sweaty items. 6 got it right.
Clue 2: "HOLD PLEASE." It holds your belongings you don't want to carry it around. 1 got it right.
Clue 1: "PUBLIC AND PRIVATE." It is a private area in a public space... in both public and private schools. 2 got it right.
Clue 1: 2 winners at $5,000.00 each.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Congratulations again to all of our winners! Go watch the all-new season of One Piece right now on Netflix! We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night!Would you like me to analyze the specific strategies used by the winners of Round 2 tonight?

clues: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello everyone and I’m excited to be back. I’m your host Hunter March. We are approaching a major milestone here on Best Guess Live—we’re getting close to 20,000 winners! That’s pretty amazing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: We’ve paid out real money 20,000 times and people have won every conceivable amount from $248 to $200,000. 20,000 is an amazing amount of winners, so stay tuned. If we pass that milestone, we’ll celebrate it! For now, let’s see how many people will take home a piece of $15,000.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. There it is! Here comes clue number one: SETTLE THE SCORE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SETTLE THE SCORE. It could be a lot of things, but I guess that’s the whole point of the show. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: SETTLE THE SCORE. “Hannah C” said tie. “Jolly Headache” said referee.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: OFTEN OVERDUE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A referee? Sometimes they’re wanted, sometimes not. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OFTEN OVERDUE. “Dr G” from Pennsylvania said karma. “Tan Doggy 44” said time out.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Clue three down. Hello to everyone playing on the Discord! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS. “Fruity Patuti” said baby. “Holly Go Brightly” said Moo Goo Gai Pan.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: RETURN. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This is your second to last clue to get your answer in! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: RETURN. “Pablo 134” from the Bronx said Uno return. “Martin the Martian” said trilogy—the return of the return!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: AN IOU FROM THE IRS. 20 seconds starts now. This is your final clue for $5,000. If you know it, type it in. If you don’t, type something fun! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a TAX REFUND.
Clue 5: "AN IOU FROM THE IRS." Well that is exactly a tax refund. 2,356 got it right.
Clue 4: "RETURN." It's the money returned to you from your tax return. 214 got it right.
Clue 3: "TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS." It is the leftover amount from what the government took out of your paycheck. 14 got it right.
Clue 2: "OFTEN OVERDUE." You paid over the amount that was due to the IRS. 27 got it right.
Clue 1: "SETTLE THE SCORE." The IRS has essentially gotten even with you once you receive it. 1 got it right.
Clue 1: 1 winner at $5,000.00 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES. 20 seconds starts now. We’re trying to hit that 20,000 winner mark! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES. “Pinkish Jade” said a hug. “Bry Pilot” said Siri. “Tamir Bob” said music.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FOUR SCORE. 20 seconds starts now. Is that how it’s spelled? I wasn’t around for Abe Lincoln. Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: FOUR SCORE. “Melm 217” said wink. “Newkerk” said math. Someone else said Connect Four.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: 8 OZ OF EARTH. 20 seconds starts now. I love this clue! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8 OZ OF EARTH. “Alaska Brook 12” said summer. “Hundrel Parrot” said parrot—maybe they know exactly what a parrot weighs?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS. 20 seconds starts now. I don’t think parrots have hands! Time’s up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS. “Pizza Bagel” said penny—Abe Lincoln’s head? “Ben There” said poker.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: GLOBAL DOMINATION IS THE GOAL. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance! Time’s up. Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It is the WORLD CUP!
Clue 5: "GLOBAL DOMINATION IS THE GOAL." The goal is to become the world champion. 857 got it right.
Clue 4: "USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS." Soccer players use their heads to touch the ball but not their hands. 125 got it right.
Clue 3: "8 OZ OF EARTH." Earth equals world and 8 ounces equals a cup. 43 got it right.
Clue 2: "FOUR SCORE." The World Cup happens every four years and you see some of the best scores. 28 got it right.
Clue 1: "SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES." The tournament has fans from all over the world who speak many different languages. 8 got it right.
Clue 1: 8 winners at $1,250.00 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to all of our winners! Check your win history for any vouchers you might have won. We’re here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. See you tomorrow! Good night! Would you like me to look for the player count information for the first clue of Round 1?

clues: WHAT'S UP WHEN IT GOES DOWN
RAIN OR SHINE IT IS DIVINE

AUDIENCES WOULD ROOT FOR HIS STUDDED JUMPSUIT
